task: H1 — bounded candidate round 1: relative-class-constrained family models
highest_claim_layer_allowed: L2/L3 (a value-bearing candidate is admitted only PROVISIONALLY and counts as L5+ ONLY if it clears the mechanical bar below; honest expectation AT_END_TO_END_NULL)

held_out_target.unit: GORILA multi-sign syllabic word tokens (t=='word', >=2 signs, all signs in the 59-value AB syllabary)
held_out_target.note: The LA word set is the HELD-OUT scoring target. Family predictions are derived from external candidate-language lexicons + a FIXED correspondence, never fitted to LA. No LA statistic informs any family's phonemes or correspondence.

non_circularity: Known Linear B syllabic values define the A<->B correspondence (script-borrowing), used to EXPAND candidate-language phonemes into predicted LA sign-value strings. They are never read off Linear A.
The D5 anonymous relative classes are used ONLY as an admission/compatibility check, never as a model input, and never to assign a value (Art. XII, Art. XV).
LB wordforms appear ONLY as the wrong-language null corpus (W).

relative_class_constraint_D5.statement: Any family that reads LA through the standard AB syllabary inherits the SAME sign->vowel/CV assignment (KU is /u/ for every family), so the D5 K=2 anonymous partition's agreement with the family-implied vowel/CV labelling is family-INDEPENDENT and equals the frozen D5 benchmark: adjMI(K2,vowel)=0.0317 perm_p=0.14, adjMI(K2,CV)=0.0395 perm_p=0.146 — NULL.
relative_class_constraint_D5.consequence: The anonymous relative classes are value-blind (D5/F4): they neither admit nor exclude any AB-based candidate family, and cannot break the symmetry between families. This is recorded, computed for the record, and does NOT gate scoring. Admission therefore rests on chronology, geography, the A-prefix morphology (E1/E3), the formula grammars (E2), the anchor evidence (G), and CV-syllabary structural fit.

scoring_protocol.primary_statistic: token-weighted match rate on the held-out multi-sign syllabic LA word set (a word matches a family iff its value-string is a predicted whole-word form, or ends with a predicted affix string)
scoring_protocol.correspondence_expansion: identical bounded expander for every family (geminate collapse; coda consonant spelled C + epenthetic dead-vowel over all family vowels; EXPAND_CAP=256 forms/lexeme); effective_n reported as total predicted forms

end_to_end_null_battery.S_order_shuffle: shuffle signs within each LA word (kills within-word form structure) — non-decisive
end_to_end_null_battery.W_wrong_language_LB: opaque DAMOS Linear B multi-sign wordforms, length-matched to LA (a REAL other language scored by the SAME matcher) — decisive
end_to_end_null_battery.R_random_prior: permute the family lexicon (same #entries/kinds, same phoneme lengths, random phonemes from the family inventory; preserves search size, destroys the specific lexemes) — decisive

scoring_protocol.decisive_FWER_family: max(p_W, p_R)  (must beat BOTH decisive nulls)
scoring_protocol.multiplicity: Holm step-down across the 5 families on the decisive FWER (Art. VIII)
scoring_protocol.leave_one_lexeme_out: worst-case decisive p after dropping each lexeme (Egyptian-channel discipline: is the signal one famous lexeme?)
scoring_protocol.random_lexicon_calibration: N_CAL=300 same-structure random lexicons per serious family; report where the real match rate sits in the random-lexicon band (percentile p) and the raw-bar false-clear rate

admission_verdict_rule.GENUINE (family counts): Holm-adjusted decisive FWER < 0.05 AND leave-one-lexeme-out worst decisive p < 0.05 AND real match rate above the random-lexicon band (percentile p < 0.05) with controlled false-clear rate (<=0.10) AND the agnostic random CONTROL does NOT clear the raw bar
admission_verdict_rule.note: A GENUINE family would still require held-out READING success and cross-site survival before any L5+ wording (Art. XV); this round can at most PROMOTE a family to Round 2, never declare a decipherment.

SEM_westSemitic.gloss: West/Common Semitic (Afro-Asiatic); Gordon 1966 / Rendsburg administrative-reading tradition

admission.chronology: PASS — West Semitic attested in the LBA E. Mediterranean; Levantine trade contact with MM/LM Crete is documented.
admission.geography: PARTIAL — no Semitic-speaking community attested on Crete; contact, not substrate.
admission.A_prefix_morphology_E1E3: WEAK — Semitic has no productive a- nominal prefix matching the LA A- affix (definite article proclitic ha-/a- is a partial analogue).
admission.formula_grammar_E2: STRONG — kull 'all/total' is the classic reading of KU-RO in the ledger TOTAL slot; this is exactly the E2 administrative anchor.
admission.script_structure: WEAK — Semitic roots are consonant-heavy with codas/clusters; an open CV syllabary spells them poorly (heavy dead-vowel epenthesis).

ANA_preGreek_Anatolian.gloss: pre-Greek Aegean / Anatolian-contact (Luwic, Indo-European); Palmer/Finkelberg -ssa/-nda substrate tradition

admission.chronology: PASS — Luwic/Anatolian IE present in W. Anatolia in the MBA-LBA, contemporaneous with Linear A.
admission.geography: PASS — the Anatolian-Aegean contact zone; -ssos/-nthos Pre-Greek toponymic substrate on Crete.
admission.A_prefix_morphology_E1E3: NEUTRAL — Anatolian has no strong a- prefix; the LA A- affix is not obviously Anatolian.
admission.formula_grammar_E2: WEAK — no compelling Anatolian reading of KU-RO/KI-RO in the TOTAL/DEFICIT slots.
admission.anchors_G: PARTIAL — the -assa toponymic suffix is compatible with Cretan substrate place-names.

lexicon.phonemes: w i y a n a

TYR_tyrsenian.gloss: Tyrsenian / Etruscan-related (pre-IE isolate family); Facchetti & Negri 2003 Minoan=Tyrrhenian hypothesis

admission.chronology: PARTIAL — Tyrsenian is a hypothesized pre-IE Aegean family (Lemnian/Etruscan cognates); Bronze-Age Aegean presence is inferred, not attested.
admission.geography: PARTIAL — an Aegean pre-Greek isolate is geographically apt; direct Cretan attestation is absent.
admission.A_prefix_morphology_E1E3: NEUTRAL — Etruscan is agglutinating/suffixing; the LA A- prefix is not a known Etruscan formant.
admission.formula_grammar_E2: WEAK — no established Tyrsenian reading of the TOTAL/DEFICIT slots.
admission.script_structure: PASS — Etruscan/Lemnian tolerate CV structure reasonably.

FIN_uralic_negctrl.gloss: Finnish / Uralic — a REAL language with a REAL lexicon and correspondence, but chronogeographically IMPOSSIBLE for MBA Crete; tests whether the machinery credits any real language by CV-shape typicality

admission.chronology: FAIL — Uralic is not present in the 2nd-millennium E. Mediterranean.
admission.geography: FAIL — no contact of any kind with Bronze-Age Crete.
admission.script_structure: PASS — Finnish is highly CV/open-syllable (which is exactly why it is a fair worst-case NEGATIVE control: maximal shape-typicality with zero historical plausibility).

CTRL_agnostic_random.gloss: agnostic random-lexicon control: 4 word + 2 suffix, phoneme lengths drawn from the serious families' entries, phonemes drawn CV-alternating from a neutral inventory; materialized deterministically at seed 20260708

admission.note: not a language; the null-model floor. If this clears the raw bar the bar is non-specific.
